# solution.py
"""TeamCollaborationManager -- an in-memory team collaboration platform.

This module implements a thread-safe, in-memory backend for a multi-user team
collaboration tool.  It supports:

* User accounts (with roles)
* Projects owned by users
* Tasks belonging to projects, optionally assigned to users, with a small
  status state-machine (NOT_STARTED -> IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED)
* Threaded messaging attached to either a project or a task, with file
  attachments
* A performance dashboard (completion rates, average completion time, peer
  ratings)
* Reporting (project progress, team performance, individual performance) with
  CSV and PDF export

Design notes
------------
* All entity identifiers are drawn from a single, globally-unique counter so
  that ids never collide across entity types.
* A single re-entrant lock is shared by the manager and every logical
  sub-manager (``users``, ``projects``, ``tasks`` ...).  Every public mutator
  and reader acquires it, which makes the whole API safe to call concurrently.
* Getters return deep copies of the stored objects so that callers can never
  mutate internal state by holding on to a returned reference.
* A ``now`` callable can be injected to make time deterministic in tests.

The implementation depends only on the Python standard library.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Enumerations
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role a user can hold within the platform."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states for a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Export formats supported by :meth:`TeamCollaborationManager.export_report`."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Logical report kinds produced by the manager."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL = "individual"


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Exception hierarchy
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when supplied input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing state."""


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Domain entities
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered user account."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project owned by a user, spanning a date range."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work inside a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    status: TaskStatus
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    started_at: Optional[datetime]
    completed_at: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime
    description: str = ""


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """File metadata attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    content: bytes = b""


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted against a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    attachments: List[Attachment]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating given to a user."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Internal helpers
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class _Component:
    """A thin logical sub-manager that shares the manager's lock.

    The public API lives on :class:`TeamCollaborationManager`; these components
    exist so that callers (and tests) can observe that every logical area of
    the system is guarded by the *same* lock instance.
    """

    __slots__ = ("_lock",)

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock


def _is_blank(value: object) -> bool:
    """Return True if *value* is not a non-empty/non-whitespace string."""

    return not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip()


def _valid_email(email: object) -> bool:
    """A deliberately small email check: non-blank and containing an ``@``."""

    return isinstance(email, str) and bool(email.strip()) and "@" in email


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Manager
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """In-memory, thread-safe team collaboration backend."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now

        # One shared, re-entrant lock for the whole system.
        self._lock = threading.RLock()

        # Logical sub-managers -- they all share the single lock.
        self.users = _Component(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Component(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Component(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Component(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Component(self._lock)
        self.reports = _Component(self._lock)

        # Storage.
        self._users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self._projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}
        self._tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self._ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}

        # Single global id source so ids are unique across all entity types.
        self._id_counter = 0

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Id / lookup helpers (assume the lock is already held)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def _alloc_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self._users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self._projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self._tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self._messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _copy(obj):
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)

    @staticmethod
    def _copy_list(objs) -> list:
        return [copy.deepcopy(o) for o in objs]

    # ================================================================== #
    # User management
    # ================================================================== #
    def register_user(self, username: str, email: str,
                      role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        """Create and store a new user account."""

        with self._lock:
            if _is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
            if not _valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("email is invalid")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            name = username.strip()
            mail = email.strip()
            for existing in self._users.values():
                if existing.username == name:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
                if existing.email == mail:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(self._alloc_id(), name, mail, role, self._now())
            self._users[user.id] = user
            return self._copy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self._users.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def update_user(self, user_id: int, username: Optional[str] = None,
                    email: Optional[str] = None,
                    role: Optional[UserRole] = None) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            new_username = user.username
            if username is not None:
                if _is_blank(username):
                    raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
                new_username = username.strip()
                for other in self._users.values():
                    if other.id != user_id and other.username == new_username:
                        raise DuplicateError("username already in use")

            new_email = user.email
            if email is not None:
                if not _valid_email(email):
                    raise ValidationError("email is invalid")
                new_email = email.strip()
                for other in self._users.values():
                    if other.id != user_id and other.email == new_email:
                        raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            user.username = new_username
            user.email = new_email
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role
            return self._copy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self._projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project and cannot be deleted")

            for task in self._tasks.values():
                if task.assignee_id == user_id and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            del self._users[user_id]

    # ================================================================== #
    # Project management
    # ================================================================== #
    def create_project(self, name: str, start_date: datetime,
                       end_date: datetime, owner_id: int,
                       description: str = "") -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            if _is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")
            self._require_user(owner_id)

            project = Project(
                self._alloc_id(),
                name.strip(),
                description or "",
                start_date,
                end_date,
                owner_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._projects[project.id] = project
            return self._copy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self._projects.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def update_project(self, project_id: int, name: Optional[str] = None,
                       start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
                       end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
                       description: Optional[str] = None) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not None:
                if _is_blank(name):
                    raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")
                new_name = name.strip()

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            new_description = (
                description if description is not None else project.description
            )

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            project.description = new_description
            return self._copy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = [
                tid for tid, task in self._tasks.items()
                if task.project_id == project_id
            ]
            task_id_set = set(task_ids)
            for tid in task_ids:
                del self._tasks[tid]

            message_ids = [
                mid for mid, msg in self._messages.items()
                if msg.project_id == project_id or msg.task_id in task_id_set
            ]
            for mid in message_ids:
                del self._messages[mid]

            del self._projects[project_id]

    # ================================================================== #
    # Task management
    # ================================================================== #
    def create_task(self, project_id: int, title: str,
                    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
                    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
                    description: str = "") -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if _is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("task title must be a non-empty string")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")

            task = Task(
                self._alloc_id(),
                project_id,
                title.strip(),
                assignee_id,
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                deadline,
                None,
                None,
                self._now(),
                description or "",
            )
            self._tasks[task.id] = task
            return self._copy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                (t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id),
                key=lambda t: t.id,
            )
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                (t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.assignee_id == assignee_id),
                key=lambda t: t.id,
            )
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return self._copy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
            return self._copy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return self._copy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int,
                     deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._copy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            elif status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None

            task.status = status
            return self._copy(task)

    # ================================================================== #
    # Messaging
    # ================================================================== #
    def post_message(self, author_id: int, body: str,
                     project_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     task_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
                     attachments: Optional[list] = None) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            targets = [t for t in (project_id, task_id) if t is not None]
            if len(targets) != 1:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id must be supplied"
                )

            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            raw_attachments = attachments or []
            has_body = isinstance(body, str) and body.strip() != ""
            if not has_body and not raw_attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message requires a body or attachments")

            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            now = self._now()
            attachment_objs = [
                Attachment(filename, len(data), author_id, now, bytes(data))
                for filename, data in raw_attachments
            ]

            message = Message(
                self._alloc_id(),
                author_id,
                body if body is not None else "",
                project_id,
                task_id,
                parent_id,
                attachment_objs,
                now,
                None,
            )
            self._messages[message.id] = message
            return self._copy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int,
                     new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not isinstance(new_body, str) or new_body.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("message body must be a non-empty string")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return self._copy(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int,
                       filename: str, data: bytes) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may attach to a message"
                )
            if _is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("filename must be a non-empty string")
            attachment = Attachment(
                filename, len(data), uploader_id, self._now(), bytes(data)
            )
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return self._copy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                (m for m in self._messages.values()
                 if m.project_id == project_id),
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            ordered = sorted(
                (m for m in self._messages.values() if m.task_id == task_id),
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return self._copy_list(ordered)

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            del self._messages[message_id]

    # ================================================================== #
    # Performance dashboard
    # ================================================================== #
    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.assignee_id == assignee_id]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_times(tasks: List[Task]) -> List[float]:
        times = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.started_at is not None
                    and task.completed_at is not None):
                times.append((task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds())
        return times

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            times = self._completion_times(self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id))
            if not times:
                return None
            return sum(times) / len(times)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id: int, ratee_id: int, score: int,
                      feedback: str = "", task_id: Optional[int] = None) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                self._alloc_id(),
                rater_id,
                ratee_id,
                score,
                feedback or "",
                task_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._ratings[rating.id] = rating
            return self._copy(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        ordered = [r for r in self._ratings.values() if r.ratee_id == user_id]
        ordered.sort(key=lambda r: r.id)
        return ordered

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count) if count else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": self._copy_list(ratings),
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            times = self._completion_times(tasks)
            avg_time = (sum(times) / len(times)) if times else None
            completion_rate = (completed / total) if total else 0.0

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ================================================================== #
    # Reports
    # ================================================================== #
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [t for t in self._tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id]
            total = len(tasks)

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            completed = 0
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            now = self._now()

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (task.deadline is not None
                        and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                        and now > task.deadline):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {"total_tasks": 0, "completed_tasks": 0},
                    )
                    bucket["total_tasks"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed_tasks"] += 1

            completion_rate = (completed / total) if total else 0.0
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                tasks = [
                    t for t in self._tasks.values()
                    if t.project_id == project_id
                ]
            else:
                tasks = list(self._tasks.values())

            by_assignee: Dict[int, List[Task]] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                by_assignee.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows = []
            for uid in sorted(by_assignee):
                user_tasks = by_assignee[uid]
                total = len(user_tasks)
                completed = sum(
                    1 for t in user_tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                )
                times = self._completion_times(user_tasks)
                avg_time = (sum(times) / len(times)) if times else None
                rows.append({
                    "user_id": uid,
                    "total_tasks": total,
                    "completed_tasks": completed,
                    "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                    "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                })

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            times = self._completion_times(tasks)
            avg_time = (sum(times) / len(times)) if times else None

            task_rows = [
                {
                    "id": t.id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Export
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def export_report(self, report: dict, fmt: ReportFormat):
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict) or "report_type" not in report:
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dict")

        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_csv(report: dict) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "user_id", "total_tasks", "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate", "average_completion_time",
            ]
            writer.writerow(header)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in header])

        elif report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", report.get("project_id", "")])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks", 0)])
            writer.writerow(
                ["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate", 0.0)]
            )
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks", 0)])
            for status, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status_{0}".format(status), count])

        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL.value:
            writer.writerow(["task_id", "title", "status", "project_id"])
            for task in report.get("tasks", []):
                writer.writerow([
                    task.get("id", ""),
                    task.get("title", ""),
                    task.get("status", ""),
                    task.get("project_id", ""),
                ])

        else:  # pragma: no cover - generic fallback
            writer.writerow(["key", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, value])

        return buffer.getvalue()

    @classmethod
    def _report_to_pdf(cls, report: dict) -> bytes:
        return cls._build_pdf(cls._report_text_lines(report))

    @staticmethod
    def _report_text_lines(report: dict) -> List[str]:
        def ascii_safe(value: object) -> str:
            return str(value).encode("ascii", "replace").decode("ascii")

        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "unknown"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} item(s)".format(key, len(value)))
                for item in value:
                    lines.append("  - {0}".format(ascii_safe(item)))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, ascii_safe(value)))
        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(text_lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
        """Build a minimal but well-formed single-page PDF document."""

        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "14 TL", "72 760 Td"]
        if not text_lines:
            text_lines = [""]
        for line in text_lines:
            content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(line)))
            content_parts.append("T*")
        content_parts.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(content_parts)
        stream_bytes = stream.encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            "<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            "<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            ("<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
             "/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"),
            "<< /Length {0} >>\nstream\n{1}\nendstream".format(
                len(stream_bytes), stream
            ),
            "<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        out = "%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(out.encode("latin-1", "replace")))
            out += "{0} 0 obj\n{1}\nendobj\n".format(index, body)

        xref_position = len(out.encode("latin-1", "replace"))
        count = len(objects) + 1
        out += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(count)
        out += "0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            out += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset)
        out += (
            "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF\n"
            .format(count, xref_position)
        )
        return out.encode("latin-1", "replace")


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Small interactive demonstration (only runs when executed directly)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    alice = manager.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    bob = manager.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com")

    project = manager.create_project(
        "Website Revamp",
        datetime(2024, 1, 1),
        datetime(2024, 3, 1),
        alice.id,
        "Redesign the marketing site",
    )

    design = manager.create_task(project.id, "Design mockups", assignee_id=bob.id)
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    manager.create_task(project.id, "Implement pages", assignee_id=bob.id)

    manager.submit_rating(alice.id, bob.id, 5, "Great work on the mockups")
    manager.post_message(alice.id, "Kickoff!", project_id=project.id)

    print("Users:", [u.username for u in manager.list_users()])
    print("Bob's dashboard:", manager.get_user_dashboard(bob.id))
    report = manager.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("Project progress:", report)
    csv_text = manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV)
    print("CSV export:\n" + csv_text)
    pdf_bytes = manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.PDF)
    print("PDF export starts with %PDF-:", pdf_bytes.startswith(b"%PDF-"))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
